reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generated the audio adhering strictly to the text script '请给我一杯水，谢谢。' without any errors, omissions, or additions. The voice quality (音质) perfectly matches the user's command for a 'hoarse' (沙哑) tone. From 00:00 to 00:03, the voice exhibits distinct acoustic characteristics of hoarseness: breathiness, vocal fry, and a strained, gravelly texture that simulates a dry or damaged throat. The delivery is slow and labored, enhancing the perception of the physical condition associated with the voice quality. The prosody is natural within the context of the specific character/state being portrayed.
reasoning_model_2: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the requested script '请给我一杯水，谢谢。', it generated: '唉，给我一杯水，谢谢。喉咙干得像砂纸磨过。' (Sigh, give me a glass of water, thanks. Throat is dry like sandpaper...). It omitted the initial '请' (Please), added an interjection '唉' (Ah/Sigh) at 00:00, and hallucinated an entire sentence explaining the condition at 00:03-00:05. Regarding the 'hoarse' (沙哑) quality, the voice sounds relatively clear and tonal, lacking the distinct gravelly or breathy texture of a truly hoarse voice; it relies on the added text to describe the dryness rather than simulating the acoustic feature itself.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the text instruction perfectly and achieved a high-quality simulation of the requested 'hoarse' voice feature (paralinguistic compliance). The audio sounds authentically raspy and strained. Model 2 failed the text constraint by adding a sigh and an extra sentence describing the throat condition (hallucination), and the voice quality itself was not acoustically hoarse, sounding instead like a normal voice acting out a scene without the required textural change.
